text.title: Cardinality and Cost Guardrails Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Cardinality and Cost Guardrails` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST publish an explicit allow-list of labels. Any label not on the list MUST be dropped at ingest.
rule.rationale: Implicit label additions cause cardinality explosions that are invisible until the bill arrives.

rule.statement: Per-user / per-request metrics MUST be rolled up via recording rules before retention; raw high-cardinality series MUST be capped at ≤ 7 days.
rule.rationale: Long retention on raw cardinality is the most expensive pattern available.

rule.statement: Each service MUST publish a daily log-volume budget; ingestion exceeding the budget for > 1 hour triggers automatic sampling.
rule.rationale: Unbounded logs from a single misbehaving service can blow a monthly budget in an afternoon.

rule.statement: Every label drop / log sampling rule MUST list a named owner; anonymous drop rules are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous drop rules silently delete signal months later when no-one knows why.

rule.statement: Cardinality + log-volume guardrails MUST be audited monthly against actual ingest costs.
rule.rationale: Drift between policy and bill is the canary for hidden cardinality.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
